intro: The pivotal question between Camunda and Apidog is how much of your setup stays portable: Camunda carries low lock-in and Apidog carries medium lock-in. Camunda is BPMN workflow and decision automation engine for orchestrating APIs and services. Apidog is all-in-one API design, mocking, testing, and documentation tool. Pick Camunda when the job is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept low lock-in; pick Apidog when it is api gateway and rate limiting and you accept medium lock-in. The data we have shows Camunda at some public pricing / roadmap detail with a polished developer experience, and Apidog at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Camunda is a poor fit for internal, simple projects, and Apidog is a poor fit for internal, simple projects. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Camunda is for BPMN workflow and decision automation engine; Apidog is for all-in-one API design; decide based on how portable the setup must stay.
verdict_a: Choose Camunda if a subscription at $100 and you want an entry-level free tier fits the budget and the work maps to BPMN workflow and decision automation engine for orchestrating APIs and services.
verdict_b: Choose Apidog if some ecosystem coupling is acceptable and the work lines up with all-in-one API design, mocking, testing, and documentation tool.

faq.a: Camunda starts at $100 on a subscription model; Apidog starts at $672/YEAR on a freemium model. That makes the sticker comparison depend on usage — a low entry price can get expensive once volume ramps, so price at your actual workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Camunda to Apidog?
faq.a: Migration is possible in either direction. Camunda carries low lock-in and Apidog carries medium lock-in, so plan for meaningful but not blocking rework. Run both in parallel before you fully cut over.

Both Camunda and Apidog rate the same on developer experience (5/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Apidog a good alternative to Camunda?
faq.a: Yes — Apidog is a reasonable alternative to Camunda for api gateway and rate limiting. The practical differences are subscription-vs-freemium billing and low-vs-medium lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Apidog as a credible swap.
